content.full_text:
Re:
FeIn PorrrIcal PnacrrcEs ConaurssroN
428 J Street o Suite 620 o Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 r Fax (916) 322-0886
April30,2004
Diane M. Fishburn
Olson, Hagel & Fishburn,LLP
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 1425
Sacramento, CA 9 581 44602
Your Request for Advice
Our FiIe No. W-04-059
Dear Ms. Fishburn:
In our telephone conversation on Monday, April 26,2004, you confirmed receipt
of my letter dated April 16, 2004, wherein we stated that your request for advice would
be withdrawn on April 26,2004, if we did not receive the additional information we had
requested by that date. You requested an additional day to provide the information,
stating that you would provide the requested information by Tuesday, April 27,2004.|t
is now Friday, April 30,2004, and we have not received the information. Therefore, we
are withdrawing your request for advice. You are welcome to resubmit your request when
you have all the information necessary for us to answer the question that you have asked.
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General CounselI
By:
yB. Armstrong
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
HBAjg
I : \Adviceltrs\W-04-05 9

sections.question_synthetic: Not applicable; this is a withdrawal letter notifying the requestor that their advice request is being withdrawn due to failure to provide requested information.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with no Q/C sections. The requestor was given an extension to April 27 but did not submit information by April 30. Letter invites resubmission once information is gathered.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Not applicable; this is a withdrawal letter notifying the requestor that their advice request is being withdrawn due to failure to provide requested information.

embedding.summary: The FPPC is withdrawing an advice request (W-04-059) from Diane M. Fishburn because she failed to provide requested additional information by the April 26, 2004 deadline, despite requesting and being granted a one-day extension to April 27, 2004.
